ws-cocoder.name: Persona sub-agent fallback when primary CLI is unavailable
ws-cocoder.summary: If Codex auth fails mid-run, fall back to a designated sub-agent persona without losing context. Currently the run hard-fails.

ws-cocoder.name: Streaming evidence for long-running QA runs
ws-cocoder.summary: Quinn's QA runs hold the transcript until they finish. Stream evidence (file diffs, decision notes) as they happen.

ws-cocoder.name: Refactor priority reorder to use shared list service
ws-cocoder.summary: Today priority order lives in the orchestrator and re-sync'd lazily. Move to a single ordered-list service.

ws-cocoder.name: Investigate Codex auth flakiness on macOS 15
ws-cocoder.summary: Three reports of Codex CLI losing auth on wake-from-sleep. Reproduce, then decide patch vs. workaround.

ws-cocoder.name: Draft v2 attach-flow with embedded Electron terminal
ws-cocoder.summary: Today the founder attaches via copy-paste iTerm command. Spec a v2 where the terminal lives in-app.

ws-cocoder.name: Persona: Translator (i18n specialist)
ws-cocoder.summary: New persona to handle i18n keys, locale review, and copy passes. Built by the team itself.

ws-cocoder.title: Wire persona sub-agent fallback
ws-cocoder.lastEvent: Quinn is auditing the fallback ladder in personaRunner.ts

evidence.label: Quinn's QA note
evidence.body: Sub-agent ladder works, but loses message history mid-handoff.

transcript.body: Decomposing priority. Three steps: (1) detect CLI auth failure (2) select fallback persona (3) replay last message in new persona context.
Step 1 complete. Added authPing() pre-flight in personaRunner. Talia's unit tests pass.
Step 2 in progress. Drafting fallback resolution algorithm in personaRunner.ts.
Concern: when we fall back, we replay the last user message but not the orchestrator's plan. Sub-agent will reason from scratch.
Flagging this for the founder. Decision needed: replay full plan, or partial?

ws-cocoder.title: Ad-hoc — audit auth surface across CLIs
ws-cocoder.lastEvent: Quinn needs a decision on whether to include Grok (not installed locally).

evidence.label: Audit scope
evidence.body: Reviewing auth surface for: Claude Code, Codex, Cursor-agent, Gemini. Open question: include Grok?

transcript.body: Ad-hoc run from Oz chat: 'audit the auth surface across all configured CLIs and report inconsistencies.'
Pulled auth flow docs for Claude Code, Codex, Cursor-agent, Gemini. Grok not installed locally.
Need decision: should the audit include Grok (since it's a known target CLI but not on this machine)?

ws-cocoder.title: Add streaming evidence for review runs
ws-cocoder.lastEvent: Merged to main. 14 files touched, 3 tests added.

evidence.label: PR #284 — Stream evidence from review runs

transcript.body: E2E scenarios pass. Approved for merge.
Checked for orchestration follow-ups; no repair needed.
Run complete. Summary posted to Slack.

ws-cocoder.title: Refactor priority reorder
ws-cocoder.lastEvent: Cursor-agent CLI auth failed. Run halted before any changes were committed.

evidence.label: cursor-agent: auth failed
evidence.body: 401 Unauthorized. Token may have expired. Re-auth from CLIs screen.

transcript.body: [error] cursor-agent exited with 401: Unauthorized. Halting run.

ws-cocoder.title: Codex auth investigation
ws-cocoder.lastEvent: Stopped by founder. Notes attached for the next attempt.

personas.name: Oz
personas.description: The headless persona that runs in the dashboard. There is exactly ONE Oz per workspace — switching workspace tabs swaps the entire Oz instance (its conversation, its watchers, its context). Multiple loaded workspaces = multiple independent Oz instances running in parallel.

personas.name: Oscar
personas.description: Owns a run end-to-end. Reads the priority, drafts the plan, dispatches Bob / Deb / Talia / Quinn, and reports back to Oz when the work is done or stuck.

personas.name: Bob
personas.description: The hands. Executes Oscar's plan, edits files in the primary root, and owns the diff. Doesn't ship without Talia + Quinn green.

personas.name: Deb
personas.description: Observes runs, triages faults, and repairs scoped orchestration machinery when the system itself blocks progress.

personas.name: Talia
personas.description: Trails Bob. Writes unit tests for new code, exercises edge cases, and flags coverage gaps before they reach Quinn.

personas.name: Quinn
personas.description: End-to-end review. Audits behaviour against the priority's intent, runs scenario suites, and surfaces decisions the founder needs to make.

clis.name: Codex
clis.errorDetail: 401 Unauthorized — token expired Tue 02:14 AM. Run `codex auth login`.

clis.name: Cursor-agent
clis.errorDetail: Token expired. Re-auth via Cursor desktop app.

clis.name: Grok CLI
clis.errorDetail: Not found on PATH. Install via `npm i -g @xai/grok-cli`.

dependencies.name: cmux
dependencies.purpose: Session multiplexer. Lets the founder attach to a run from anywhere and resume after disconnects.
dependencies.note: The terminal host CoCoder drives over its Unix socket (ADR-0002). The only system dependency.

ws-cocoder.body: Welcome back. You have **2 runs active** and **1 needs your call** on Quinn's audit. Top of the priority list is unchanged: persona sub-agent fallback.

Run-1 is 62% in. Bob finished step 1 (auth-ping pre-flight) and step 2 (fallback resolution) is drafted. Quinn raised a concern: when we fall back to a sub-agent, we replay the user message but not Oscar's plan.
Decision needed: should fallback replay the **full plan** (slower, more context) or **partial** (last 2 messages, faster, may lose framing)?

ws-vault.body: No active runs. Nothing on the priority list yet. Want to seed one?

ws-lumen.body: Lumen workspace is quiet. Last run completed Friday.
